samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Design an 8-bit multiplier using pipelined processing. Implement the design using sequential logic blocks.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal for chip reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling the multiplication process.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input signal with a width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input signal with a width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating the completion of the multiplication process.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">State IDLE = 0.</state>
        <state id="LOAD">State LOAD = 1.</state>
        <state id="CALCULATE">State CALCULATE = 2.</state>
        <state id="OUTPUT">State OUTPUT = 3.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[3:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand input.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier input.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the seventh partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the eighth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the seventh partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the eighth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product output.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set all registers to zero
                mul_en_out_reg <= 4'd0;
                mul_a_reg <= 8'd0;
                mul_b_reg <= 8'd0;
                temp[0] <= 16'd0;
                temp[1] <= 16'd0;
                temp[2] <= 16'd0;
                temp[3] <= 16'd0;
                temp[4] <= 16'd0;
                temp[5] <= 16'd0;
                temp[6] <= 16'd0;
                temp[7] <= 16'd0;
                sum[0] <= 16'd0;
                sum[1] <= 16'd0;
                sum[2] <= 16'd0;
                sum[3] <= 16'd0;
                sum[4] <= 16'd0;
                sum[5] <= 16'd0;
                sum[6] <= 16'd0;
                sum[7] <= 16'd0;
                mul_out_reg <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Update the output enable signal register
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[2:0], mul_en_in};
                
                // Update the multiplicand and multiplier registers
                if (mul_en_in == 1'b1) begin
                    mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                    mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                end
                
                // Generate partial products based on the multiplier bits
                temp[0] <= mul_b_reg[0] ? {8'd0, mul_a_reg} : 16'd0;
                temp[1] <= mul_b_reg[1] ? {7'd0, mul_a_reg, 1'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[2] <= mul_b_reg[2] ? {6'd0, mul_a_reg, 2'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[3] <= mul_b_reg[3] ? {5'd0, mul_a_reg, 3'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[4] <= mul_b_reg[4] ? {4'd0, mul_a_reg, 4'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[5] <= mul_b_reg[5] ? {3'd0, mul_a_reg, 5'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[6] <= mul_b_reg[6] ? {2'd0, mul_a_reg, 6'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[7] <= mul_b_reg[7] ? {1'd0, mul_a_reg, 7'd0} : 16'd0;
                
                // Calculate partial sums based on the generated partial products
                sum[0] <= temp[0];
                sum[1] <= temp[1] + temp[2];
                sum[2] <= temp[3] + temp[4];
                sum[3] <= temp[5] + temp[6];
                sum[4] <= temp[7];
                sum[5] <= sum[1] + sum[2];
                sum[6] <= sum[3] + sum[4];
                sum[7] <= sum[5] + sum[6];
                
                // Accumulate the final product output
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[7];
            end
        end
        
        // Assign the output enable signal to the most significant bit of the output enable register
        assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[3];
        
        // Assign the final product output to the output port
        assign mul_out = mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 16'd0;
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Sequential logic blocks are implemented using the always @(*) construct. The output enable signal is assigned to the most significant bit of the output enable register. The final product output is assigned to the output port based on the output enable signal.</implementation>
endmodule
